headline: Producers Are Looking Far Afield for Oil. It’s ‘An Old-Fashioned Land Grab.’
summary: Oil and gas companies have been ramping up their search for new opportunities outside the U.S. for quite a while, and the war in Iran will likely accelerate their hunt in far-off regions.  It’s “an old-fashioned land grab,” Bob Fryklund, S&P Global’s chief upstream energy strategist, said of the industry’s rush to acquire new acreage.  “Each company has a different strategy,” he noted while speaking this week at CERAWeek, an annual energy conference in Houston.

headline: Halliburton (HAL) Is Up 6.7% After First Fully Automated Offshore Well in Guyana - What's Changed
summary: In March 2026, Halliburton, together with ExxonMobil, Sekal, Noble and the Wells Alliance Guyana team, delivered the industry’s first fully automated geological well placement with complete rig automation in offshore Guyana, using LOGIX orchestration, EarthStar ultra-deep resistivity and Sekal’s DrillTronics in an integrated closed-loop system. This project showed how unifying subsurface interpretation, rig automation and real-time optimization can materially improve execution efficiency,...

headline: Exxon Mobil Weighs New Zealand Exit To Refocus On Advantaged Assets
summary: Exxon Mobil (NYSE:XOM) has reportedly hired Goldman Sachs to explore a potential exit from its New Zealand operations. The move would follow recent asset sales in Singapore and continue a focus on higher return advantaged assets. A New Zealand exit would further reshape Exxon Mobil's global footprint toward larger, more profitable projects. For you as an investor, this potential exit sits within a broader repositioning of Exxon Mobil's portfolio as a global integrated energy company. The...

headline: How 4 Dividend Growth ETFs Beat Inflation While the Fed Keeps Cutting Rates
summary: With the Federal Reserve having cut rates from 4.5% to 3.75% over the past six months, the calculus for income investors is shifting. Cash and money market yields are drifting lower, while the 10-year Treasury sits near 4.34%, a level that demands dividend equity strategies earn their keep through growth, not just yield. Dividend growth ... How 4 Dividend Growth ETFs Beat Inflation While the Fed Keeps Cutting Rates
